septal
  • SQP algorithm
  • View page source

SQP algorithm

Problem formulation

The JAX SQP solver targets the parametric NLP:

\[\min_{x \in \mathbb{R}^n} \; f(x, p) \quad \text{s.t.} \quad \ell \le g(x, p) \le r, \quad lb \le x \le ub\]

where \(p \in \mathbb{R}^m\) is a fixed parameter vector. When \(m = 0\) the problem is non-parametric. Setting \(\ell_i = r_i\) defines an equality constraint; \(\ell_i = -\infty\) or \(r_i = +\infty\) define one-sided inequalities.

Outer SQP iteration

At iterate \((x_k, \lambda_k)\) the solver performs:

Step 1 — Evaluate functions and derivatives

\[\begin{split}f_k,\; \nabla f_k &= \texttt{jax.value\_and\_grad}(f)(x_k, p) \\ g_k &= g(x_k, p) \\ J_k &= \texttt{jax.jacfwd}(g)(x_k, p) \in \mathbb{R}^{n_g \times n}\end{split}\]

Step 2 — Solve the QP subproblem for search direction \(d_k\):

\[\begin{split}\min_{d} \; &\tfrac{1}{2} d^\top H_k d + \nabla f_k^\top d \\ \text{s.t.} \; &\ell - g_k \le J_k d \le r - g_k \quad \text{(linearised constraints)} \\ &lb - x_k \le d \le ub - x_k \quad \text{(box constraints on step)}\end{split}\]

This is an inner QP solved via OSQP-style ADMM (see QP subproblem solver).

Step 3 — Penalty update

\[\rho_{k+1} = \max\!\left(\|\lambda_k\|_\infty + \varepsilon_\rho,\; \rho_k\right)\]

Step 4 — Line search on the L1 merit function:

\[\varphi(x;\rho) = f(x,p) + \rho \sum_i \max(g_i(x,p) - r_i,\, 0) + \max(\ell_i - g_i(x,p),\, 0)\]

Armijo backtracking (lax.while_loop): find \(\alpha_k = \beta^j\) such that

\[\varphi(x_k + \alpha_k d_k) \;\le\; \bar\varphi_k + c\,\alpha_k\, D\varphi_k\]

where \(\bar\varphi_k\) is the reference merit (max over last \(M\) iterates for the non-monotone variant) and \(D\varphi_k\) is the directional derivative.

Step 5 — Primal and dual update

\[x_{k+1} = x_k + \alpha_k d_k, \qquad \lambda_{k+1} = \lambda_\text{QP}\]

Step 6 — Hessian update (damped BFGS, Powell 1978):

\[\begin{split}s_k &= x_{k+1} - x_k \\ y_k &= \nabla_x L(x_{k+1}, \lambda_{k+1}, p) - \nabla_x L(x_k, \lambda_k, p) \\ \theta_k &= \begin{cases} 1 & \text{if } s_k^\top y_k \ge 0.2\, s_k^\top H_k s_k \\ \dfrac{0.8\, s_k^\top H_k s_k}{s_k^\top H_k s_k - s_k^\top y_k} & \text{otherwise} \end{cases} \\ r_k &= \theta_k y_k + (1-\theta_k) H_k s_k \\ H_{k+1} &= H_k - \frac{H_k s_k s_k^\top H_k}{s_k^\top H_k s_k} + \frac{r_k r_k^\top}{s_k^\top r_k}\end{split}\]

Step 7 — Convergence check

\[\begin{split}\|\,\text{proj}_{[lb,ub]}(x - \nabla_x L) - x\,\|_\infty &\le \varepsilon_\text{stat} \quad \text{(projected-gradient stationarity)} \\ \max_i\,\max\bigl(g_i - r_i,\; \ell_i - g_i,\; 0\bigr) &\le \varepsilon_\text{feas} \quad \text{(L}\infty\text{ feasibility)}\end{split}\]

QP subproblem solver

The QP subproblem is cast into OSQP standard form:

\[\min_{d} \; \tfrac{1}{2} d^\top Q d + c^\top d \qquad \text{s.t.} \quad l \le A d \le u\]

where

\[\begin{split}Q = H_k + \varepsilon I, \quad c = \nabla f_k, \quad A = \begin{bmatrix} J_k \\ I_n \end{bmatrix}, \quad l = \begin{bmatrix} \ell - g_k \\ lb - x_k \end{bmatrix}, \quad u = \begin{bmatrix} r - g_k \\ ub - x_k \end{bmatrix}\end{split}\]

Solved via three stages:

  1. Ruiz diagonal equilibration — scales rows/columns of \((Q, A)\) to unit \(\infty\)-norm, removing scale imbalance before ADMM.

  2. ADMM (lax.scan, fixed iterations) — standard OSQP-ADMM with adaptive \(\rho\), over-relaxation, and per-row equality boosting.

  3. OSQP-style polishing — classifies the active set from the ADMM solution, then solves a reduced KKT system with iterative refinement to recover high accuracy.

Robustness features

Exact Lagrangian Hessian

When use_exact_hessian=True, the BFGS approximation is replaced by the exact second-order information:

\[\nabla^2_{xx} L(x, \lambda, p) = \nabla^2 f(x,p) + \sum_{i=1}^{n_g} \lambda_i\, \nabla^2 g_i(x,p)\]

computed via jax.hessian. An inertia correction \(\tau I\) is applied to guarantee positive definiteness before passing to the QP solver:

\[\tau = \max(\delta_\text{min},\; -\lambda_\text{min}(H) + \delta)\]

Ruiz equilibration

Finds diagonal matrices \(D\) (columns) and \(E\) (rows) such that after scaling

\[Q_s = D Q D, \quad A_s = E A D, \quad c_s = Dc, \quad l_s = El, \quad u_s = Eu\]

all row/column \(\infty\)-norms are approximately 1. After solving: \(x = D\tilde{x}\), \(y = E\tilde{y}\).

OSQP polishing

After ADMM, classifies constraints as lower-active, upper-active, or inactive, then solves the reduced KKT system (Stellato et al. 2020, Eq. 30):

\[\begin{split}\begin{bmatrix} Q + \sigma I & A^\top \mathrm{diag}(a) \\ \mathrm{diag}(a) A & \mathrm{diag}((1-a)\varepsilon) \end{bmatrix} \begin{bmatrix} \hat{x} \\ \hat{y} \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} -c \\ a \odot b_A \end{bmatrix}\end{split}\]

where \(a_i = 1\) for active constraints. Three acceptance gates prevent the polished solution from degrading the ADMM result.

Non-monotone line search

The reference merit is \(\bar\varphi_k = \max_{j \in W_k} \varphi_j\) over the last \(M\) iterates (Grippo et al. 1986), allowing temporary merit increases to escape saddle points. Controlled by nonmonotone_window (set to 1 for standard Armijo).

Stagnation reset

After stagnation_patience consecutive iterations with \(\alpha_k < \alpha_\text{tol}\), the solver resets \(H \leftarrow \sigma_0 I\) and \(\rho \leftarrow \rho_0\). This breaks out of regions where BFGS has accumulated bad curvature.

lax.scan vs lax.while_loop

sqp_solve_scan()

sqp_solve_single()

Iterations

Always max_iter

Early exit on convergence

jax.vmap-compatible

Yes

No (variable iteration count)

jax.jit-compilable

Yes

Yes

Recommended for

Batched / GPU solves

Single or sequential multi-start

Converged iterates in scan mode are frozen via masked tree-map:

jax.tree.map(
    lambda new, old: jnp.where(state.converged, old, new),
    new_state, state
)
